name: What should I expect during siding tear-off day?

mainEntity.name: What happens on the first day when old siding is removed?
mainEntity.text: What happens on the first day when old siding is removed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tear-off day is often the most disruptive part of a siding project. Crews typically protect nearby surfaces, remove the old siding, expose the wall assembly, and inspect for damage around windows, doors, corners, and roof intersections. This is when hidden rot, bad flashing, or water intrusion problems often become visible. The process can create noise, dust, and debris, so homeowners should expect a messy but important stage of the job. A quality contractor should keep the site organized, protect landscaping, and communicate quickly if unexpected repairs are needed. If the estimate included an allowance for concealed damage, this is when that allowance is often used. It helps to ask in advance how the contractor documents findings and how they approve extra work before proceeding.
acceptedAnswer.description: Tear-off day usually includes protecting landscaping, removing old siding, exposing the wall sheathing, and inspecting for hidden damage. Homeowners should expect noise, dust, debris, and the possibility of change orders if rot or flashing failures are discovered.
